---
title: "How to Back Up F-Stop To Include Tags"
date: 2012-02-27
forum: New to Ubuntu
---

### Post by onaridge on 2012-02-27
I want to back up my F-Stop catalog of photos because I have done a lot of tagging and there are over 10,000 photos. I found some info on where that file is to be found but it is not there. How can I find it and how exactly do I back it up so that it can be restored should I have a failure. My plan is to stick the file on my external back up drive when I find it. Is that all I have to do?

---

### Post by lechien73 on 2012-02-27
My F-Spot files are stored in ~/.config/f-spot so backing up that folder - including the photos.db file would work.

You could try running f-spot from the command line using strace:

```
strace -o ~/f-spot.log -e open f-spot
```

Then examine the ~/f-spot.log file to see the config path for f-spot's files.

---

### Post by onaridge on 2012-02-27
Where is the F-Spot log file?

---

### Post by lechien73 on 2012-02-27
It will be created in your home directory if you run f-spot with the strace command I gave you.

---

### Post by onaridge on 2012-02-28
This is where my pictures are:

/home/loren/Pictures/2010
/home/loren/Pictures/2011
/home/loren/Pictures/2012
/home/loren/Pictures/Photos
/home/loren/Pictures/digikam4.db
/home/loren/Pictures/thumbnails-digikam.db

So is the **digikam4.db** file the db file that I need to backup in order to get my tags intact, along with my **user/lib/f-spot** file? Then if I have to reinstall F-spot I just put the above two files back? Why are my files ina  different place then everyone elses?

---

### Post by lechien73 on 2012-02-28
The files you need to backup are in /home/loren/.config/f-spot/ as can be seen from this output:

```
open("/home/loren/.config/f-spot/addin-db-001/hosts", O_RDONLY|O_NONBLOCK|O_LARGEFILE|O_DIRECTORY|O_CLOE XEC) = 19
open("/home/loren/.config/f-spot/addin-db-001/hosts/f-spot.addins", O_RDONLY|O_LARGEFILE) = 19
open("/home/loren/.config/f-spot/addin-db-001/fdb-lock", O_RDONLY|O_CREAT|O_LARGEFILE, 0666) = 19
open("/home/loren/.config/f-spot/addin-db-001/fdb-lock", O_RDONLY|O_CREAT|O_LARGEFILE, 0666) = 19
open("/home/loren/.config/f-spot/addin-db-001/host-index", O_RDONLY|O_LARGEFILE) = 20
open("/home/loren/.config/f-spot/addin-db-001/fdb-lock", O_RDONLY|O_CREAT|O_LARGEFILE, 0666) = 19
open("/home/loren/.config/f-spot/addin-db-001/fdb-lock", O_RDONLY|O_CREAT|O_LARGEFILE, 0666) = 19
open("/home/loren/.config/f-spot/addin-db-001/fdb-lock", O_RDONLY|O_CREAT|O_LARGEFILE, 0666) = 19
open("/home/loren/.config/f-spot/addin-db-001/fdb-lock", O_RDONLY|O_CREAT|O_LARGEFILE, 0666) = 19
```

The .config directory is hidden. To view hidden files in Nautilus (the file browser), open your home directory and press Ctrl+H. You will then be able to browse to /home/loren/.config/f-spot/ and backup the files and folders in there.

As far as I know, photos.db in that directory is where the tags are stored. Digikam is a different photo management program.

By the way - if you're posting large listings again, could you please use CODE tags (the **#** button on the post toolbar)? It makes it much easier to read. Thanks :)

---

### Post by onaridge on 2012-02-28
Thank you thank you Lechien! Sorry about the big paste. 
I was pressing control H in the wrong place which is why I couldn't find the config folder. Thanks for spelling it out as that was what was needed. So if I ever have to reinstall F-Spot due to a crash or something, I will just put the backed up f-spot folder that resides in the config folder, into the newly installed f-spot folder and replace it, correct?  

BTW I went to Trinity College a million years ago.

---

### Post by lechien73 on 2012-02-28
No problem - and yes, you're correct. As long as the photo paths remain the same, you can just restore the .config/f-spot folder to get the tags back.

Trinity, eh? I'm within walking distance of the city centre campus. I'm Dublin 3, just north of the Liffey, Trinity is Dublin 2.

If you're happy with the answer, could you please use the **Thread Tools** menu and mark this as [SOLVED]?

Many thanks,

---
